title: Salesforce Multi-Touch Attribution Model
summary: Designed a weighted Salesforce attribution model connecting marketing, event, sales and organisational engagement with commercial outcomes.
details: Created a structured multi-touch attribution approach for a complex education-sector buying journey. The model considered recent interactions across campaigns, events, referrals, sales activity and organisational relationships, then distributed influence across applicable channels.

title: Local-Authority Account Hierarchy and Pricing Model
summary: Designed a Salesforce account structure that represented relationships between local authorities, organisations, contacts, leads and commercial agreements.
details: Developed a parent-account and relationship model that reflected the influence local authorities could have across multiple education organisations. The structure supported contract pricing, segmentation, governance and more accurate commercial reporting.
featuredMetric: Applied across 1,000+ accounts and 20,000+ leads and contacts.

title: Salesforce Flow Automation for Commercial Processes
summary: Built Salesforce automation to improve data consistency, reduce manual administration and support repeatable commercial workflows.
details: Used Salesforce Flow to automate commercial processes involving accounts, contacts, leads, opportunities and related organisational data. The work reduced dependence on manual updates and helped ensure that key information was handled consistently.
featuredMetric: Created repeatable workflows across multiple CRM record types.

title: Buying-Committee and Organisational Relationship Mapping
summary: Structured CRM data around the people and organisations influencing complex public-sector and education-sector purchasing decisions.
details: Designed a relationship-led approach to CRM data that went beyond individual leads and contacts. The model accounted for stakeholders, organisations, local authorities and wider buying committees involved in long and complex commercial journeys.
featuredMetric: Connected individual engagement with wider organisational influence.

title: CRM Data Governance and Permission Architecture
summary: Improved Salesforce governance through clearer ownership, permissions, field controls and structured data-management practices.
details: Developed governance principles for how commercial and marketing data should be created, accessed and maintained. The work included consideration of permissions, user responsibilities, field consistency and the risks created by uncontrolled CRM changes.
featuredMetric: Established clearer controls for CRM access and data quality.

title: Lead, Contact and Account Data Standardisation
summary: Standardised commercial data across Salesforce leads, contacts and accounts to improve segmentation, reporting and operational reliability.
details: Reviewed how information was stored across different Salesforce objects and introduced more consistent structures and definitions. This supported cleaner segmentation, stronger reporting and more reliable movement between leads, contacts, accounts and opportunities.
featuredMetric: Improved consistency across key commercial CRM objects.

title: Salesforce Campaign Influence and Revenue Reporting
summary: Connected campaign engagement with Salesforce commercial records to improve visibility of marketing influence and revenue contribution.
details: Structured campaign and engagement information so that marketing activity could be assessed alongside leads, contacts, opportunities and purchases. This created a stronger foundation for revenue reporting and campaign-performance analysis.
featuredMetric: Linked marketing engagement with opportunity and revenue data.

title: CRM Migration and Legacy Data Rationalisation
summary: Reviewed and rationalised legacy commercial data before moving viable records into a more controlled Salesforce and marketing environment.
details: Assessed legacy CRM and marketing records to identify duplication, poor-quality data, unusable contacts and governance risks. The resulting rationalisation supported a cleaner platform migration and reduced the volume of unreliable data entering the new operating environment.
featuredMetric: Reduced approximately 30,000 legacy contacts to 19,000 viable prospects.

title: Sales and Marketing Alignment Through Salesforce
summary: Improved the connection between sales and marketing by creating shared Salesforce structures, definitions and commercial workflows.
details: Worked across marketing and commercial requirements to reduce separation between campaign engagement and sales activity. Shared CRM structures helped both functions work from more consistent information about prospects, organisations, opportunities and customer journeys.
featuredMetric: Created shared commercial data structures across sales and marketing.

title: Salesforce Opportunity and Revenue Forecasting Framework
summary: Improved the structure of Salesforce opportunity data to support clearer pipeline analysis, forecasting and commercial decision-making.
details: Developed a more structured approach to opportunity and revenue information, considering stage progression, account relationships, buying journeys and wider commercial influence. The work strengthened the CRM foundation required for more dependable pipeline and forecast reporting.
featuredMetric: Created a stronger data foundation for pipeline and revenue forecasting.
